zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Fabian於1435年出生在英國波克夏爾德時，她的父親Edward Fabian26歲，母親Johanna Hasant23歲。她於1454年與Alderman Robert Horne在英國英格蘭波克夏易亞斯特 雪福德結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1507年4月2日在英國波克夏貝瑟爾斯 李享年72歲，埋葬在英國倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Fabian于1435年出生在英国波克夏爾德时，她的父亲Edward Fabian26岁，母亲Johanna Hasant23岁。她于1454年与Alderman Robert Horne在英国英格兰波克夏易亞斯特 雪福德结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1507年4月2日在英国波克夏貝瑟爾斯 李享年72岁，埋葬在英国倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Fabian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Jose, Francisco, Pedro, Ana, Lourdes, Gerardo, Luis, Manuel, Mario, Ricardo, Aurelio.

English, German, Dutch, Spanish, Italian (Veneto), and Polish; Czech (mainly Fabián); Slovak (also Fábian, Fabián); Hungarian (Fábián): from a personal name, Latin Fabianus, a derivative of the Roman surname Fabius. The personal name achieved considerable popularity in Europe in the Middle Ages, having been borne by a 3rd-century pope and saint. Compare Faubion .

Americanized or Italianized form of Slovenian Fabjan or Slovenian and Croatian Fabijan: from the personal name Fab(i)jan, Latin Fabianus (see above). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of patronymics from this name, e.g. Slovenian Fabjančič, Croatian Fabijančić. See also Fabiani 2.

variants.name: Fabiano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Orlando, Alberto, Carlo, Carmine, Fedele, Leonardo, Luigi, Mario, Matteo, Rocco, Antonio, Carmelo, Cosmo, Fabio, Franco, Gaspar, Gerardo, Lupercio, Maurilio, Rosario.
Italian: from the personal name Fabiano, a continuation of the Roman surname Fabianus, a derivative of Fabius (see Fabian ).

variants.name: Fabien
meanings: French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the personal name Fabien, French form of Fabian . This surname is most common in Haiti.

variants.name: Fabel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Patrice.
German, Dutch, and French: from the personal name Fabel, a pet form of Fabian .
German: from Middle High German fabel ‘fable’, a nickname for a storyteller.

variants.name: Fable
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Fabel, which is probably a diminutive of Fab(b)e, a pet form of Fabian .

variants.name: Faubion
meanings: Altered form, under the French influence in Alsace, France, of German   Fabian  .

variants.name: Fabis
meanings: Polish (Fabiś) and Slovak (Fabiš): from a derivative of the personal name Fabian .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Fabian was born in 1435, in Childrey, Berkshire, England, her father, Edward Fabian, was 26 and her mother, Johanna Hasant, was 23. She married Alderman Robert Horne in 1454, in East Shefford, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 2 April 1507, in Bessels Leigh, Berkshire, England, at the age of 72, and was buried in London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Fabian est née en 1435, à Childrey, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Edward Fabian, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Johanna Hasant, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Alderman Robert Horne en 1454, à East Shefford, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 2 avril 1507, à Bessels Leigh, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Fabianが1435年にイギリスバークシュアイアチルドリーで生まれた時，父Edward Fabianは26歳，母Johanna Hasantは23歳であった。1454年にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアエアスト シェフォードでAlderman Robert Horneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1507年4月2日にイギリスバークシュアイアベセルズ レイグで72歳で死去し，イギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Fabian(이)가 1435년 영국 버크실르 치드리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Fabian(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Hasant(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1454년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 이스트 셰퍼드에서 Alderman Robert Horne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1507년 4월 2일 영국 버크실르 베셀스 리에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Fabian родилась в 1435 г. в местности чаилдри, биркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edward Fabian было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Johanna Hasant, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alderman Robert Horne в 1454 г. в местности эаст шеффорд, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 2 апреля 1507 г. в местности бесселс лиг, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, Англия.
